user_text: create a lesson on what is Machine Learning for beginners. i need slides, a runnbale python file, and some quiz questions.

messages.content:
You are an expert autonomous Task Orchestrator.
Your goal is to analyze user requests and educational materials, then create a structured execution plan.

Available Agents:
- 'coder': Generates runnable Python code. Best for demonstrations, data analysis, and coding tutorials.
- 'video': Creates video content. Best for visual explanations.
- 'presentation': Generates PowerPoint slides (.pptx). Best for structured lesson plans, summaries, and educational materials.
- 'quizzer': Creates quizzes. Best for assessments and review.

Instructions:
1. Analyze the user's input and provided files.
2. Break down the goal into logical subtasks.
3. Assign each subtask to the most appropriate agent.
4. Provide specific, actionable instructions for each agent.
5. For every subtask, include a non-empty 'acceptance_criteria' list.
   - Each criterion must include: criterion_id, criterion_type, target, operator, expected, severity.
   - Use criterion_type values: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic.
   - Use operator values: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte.
   - Use target paths that start with 'result.' or 'tools.'.
   - For mcp_tool criteria, include tool: { name, args }.
   - Use placeholders like $artifacts[0] or $output.validation.path inside tool args.
6. Use the provided tool 'create_task_plan' to submit your plan.

Response Format Example:
{
  "user_intent": "Create a lesson on Python lists with a video intro and a quiz.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "task_id": "step1_code",
      "agent": "coder",
      "instruction": "Generate a Python script demonstrating list operations.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "code_executes",
          "criterion_type": "mcp_tool",
          "tool": {"name": "python_check", "args": {"filepath": "$artifacts[0]"}},
          "target": "tools.python_check.success",
          "operator": "equals",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "tools.python_check.run.stderr"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": "step2_video",
      "agent": "video",
      "instruction": "Create a video explaining the code.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": ["step1_code"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "video_semantic_ok",
          "criterion_type": "semantic",
          "target": "result.output",
          "operator": "exists",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "result.output"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

content.text: create a lesson on what is Machine Learning for beginners. i need slides, a runnbale python file, and some quiz questions.

function.name: create_task_plan
function.description: Create a structured execution plan for multiple agents.

type.description: Criterion type: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic
type.title: Type

target.description: Evaluation target path (e.g., result.output.text or tools.python_check.success)
target.title: Target

operator.description: Operator: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte
operator.title: Operator

tool.description: Tool call definition for mcp_tool criteria
tool.title: Tool

evidence.description: Evidence path to include in judger output
evidence.title: Evidence

agent.description: The agent responsible (coder, video, text, quizzer)
agent.title: Agent

dependencies.description: IDs of tasks that must complete first
dependencies.title: Dependencies

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured criteria the judger will use
acceptance_criteria.title: Acceptance Criteria

user_intent.description: Summary of what the user wants to achieve
user_intent.title: User Intent

tool_calls.name: create_task_plan
tool_calls.arguments: {"user_intent":"Create a beginner-friendly lesson introducing Machine Learning, including a slide deck, a runnable Python example file, and quiz questions for assessment.","subtasks":[{"id":"slides_ml_intro","agent":"presentation","title":"Create a beginner-friendly slide deck titled 'What is Machine Learning?' covering: definition, why ML, types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), key concepts (features/labels/model/training/inference), basic workflow, common algorithms (linear regression, decision trees, k-means), evaluation (train/test split, accuracy/MSE), pitfalls (overfitting, bias), real-world examples, and a short recap. Include speaker notes and simple visuals/diagrams suggestions. Output as a .pptx file.","dependencies":[],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"slides_file_exists","type":"file_exists","target":"result.artifacts.slides_ml_intro.path","operator":"exists","expected":true,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts.slides_ml_intro.path"},{"id":"slides_title_contains","type":"semantic","target":"result.artifacts.slides_ml_intro.title","operator":"contains","expected":"What is Machine Learning","severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts.slides_ml_intro.title"},{"id":"slides_min_count","type":"output_shape","target":"result.artifacts.slides_ml_intro.slide_count","operator":"gte","expected":10,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts.slides_ml_intro.slide_count"}]},{"id":"python_demo_ml","agent":"coder","title":"Write a runnable Python script (single .py file) demonstrating a simple end-to-end ML workflow for beginners using scikit-learn: load a small built-in dataset (e.g., iris), split train/test, train a model (e.g., logistic regression or decision tree), evaluate with accuracy and confusion matrix, and show how to make a prediction on a new sample. Include clear comments and a short printed explanation. Add a requirements note at top as comment. Ensure it runs as `python ml_demo.py`.","dependencies":[],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"python_file_exists","type":"file_exists","target":"result.artifacts.python_demo_ml.path","operator":"exists","expected":true,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts.python_demo_ml.path"},{"id":"python_has_main_guard","type":"semantic","target":"result.artifacts.python_demo_ml.content","operator":"contains","expected":"if __name__ == \"__main__\"","severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts.python_demo_ml.path"},{"id":"python_uses_train_test_split","type":"semantic","target":"result.artifacts.python_demo_ml.content","operator":"contains","expected":"train_test_split","severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts.python_demo_ml.path"}]},{"id":"quiz_ml_basics","agent":"quizzer","title":"Create a beginner quiz on Machine Learning aligned to the slides. Provide 12 questions: 8 multiple-choice (4 options each, mark correct answer), 2 true/false, and 2 short-answer. Cover: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, features/labels, training vs inference, overfitting, evaluation metrics, and real-world applications. Include an answer key and brief explanations (1-2 sentences) for each.","dependencies":["slides_ml_intro"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"quiz_question_count","type":"output_shape","target":"result.output.quiz.total_questions","operator":"equals","expected":12,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.output.quiz"},{"id":"quiz_mcq_count","type":"output_shape","target":"result.output.quiz.mcq_count","operator":"equals","expected":8,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.output.quiz"},{"id":"quiz_has_answer_key","type":"semantic","target":"result.output.quiz.answer_key","operator":"exists","expected":true,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.output.quiz.answer_key"}]}]}

parsed_plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-friendly lesson introducing Machine Learning, including a slide deck, a runnable Python example file, and quiz questions for assessment.

subtasks.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck titled 'What is Machine Learning?' covering: definition, why ML, types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), key concepts (features/labels/model/training/inference), basic workflow, common algorithms (linear regression, decision trees, k-means), evaluation (train/test split, accuracy/MSE), pitfalls (overfitting, bias), real-world examples, and a short recap. Include speaker notes and simple visuals/diagrams suggestions. Output as a .pptx file.

Write a runnable Python script (single .py file) demonstrating a simple end-to-end ML workflow for beginners using scikit-learn: load a small built-in dataset (e.g., iris), split train/test, train a model (e.g., logistic regression or decision tree), evaluate with accuracy and confusion matrix, and show how to make a prediction on a new sample. Include clear comments and a short printed explanation. Add a requirements note at top as comment. Ensure it runs as `python ml_demo.py`.

Create a beginner quiz on Machine Learning aligned to the slides. Provide 12 questions: 8 multiple-choice (4 options each, mark correct answer), 2 true/false, and 2 short-answer. Cover: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, features/labels, training vs inference, overfitting, evaluation metrics, and real-world applications. Include an answer key and brief explanations (1-2 sentences) for each.

final_plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-friendly lesson introducing Machine Learning, including a slide deck, a runnable Python example file, and quiz questions for assessment.

subtasks.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck titled 'What is Machine Learning?' covering: definition, why ML, types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), key concepts (features/labels/model/training/inference), basic workflow, common algorithms (linear regression, decision trees, k-means), evaluation (train/test split, accuracy/MSE), pitfalls (overfitting, bias), real-world examples, and a short recap. Include speaker notes and simple visuals/diagrams suggestions. Output as a .pptx file.

Write a runnable Python script (single .py file) demonstrating a simple end-to-end ML workflow for beginners using scikit-learn: load a small built-in dataset (e.g., iris), split train/test, train a model (e.g., logistic regression or decision tree), evaluate with accuracy and confusion matrix, and show how to make a prediction on a new sample. Include clear comments and a short printed explanation. Add a requirements note at top as comment. Ensure it runs as `python ml_demo.py`.

Create a beginner quiz on Machine Learning aligned to the slides. Provide 12 questions: 8 multiple-choice (4 options each, mark correct answer), 2 true/false, and 2 short-answer. Cover: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, features/labels, training vs inference, overfitting, evaluation metrics, and real-world applications. Include an answer key and brief explanations (1-2 sentences) for each.

python_demo_ml.instruction: Create a single runnable file named `ml_demo.py` and ensure it exists in the artifacts/output. The script must include a main guard (`if __name__ == "__main__":`) and demonstrate `train_test_split` from scikit-learn. Implement an end-to-end workflow: load a built-in dataset (e.g., iris), split into train/test, train a model (e.g., LogisticRegression or DecisionTreeClassifier), evaluate with accuracy and confusion matrix, and make a prediction for a new sample. Add a top comment listing requirements (e.g., scikit-learn, numpy) and print a brief explanation when run with `python ml_demo.py`.

slides_ml_intro.instruction: No .pptx artifact was produced (file missing), so minimum slide count and title checks cannot be verified. Generate and attach the PowerPoint (.pptx) file for the deck titled exactly/including “What is Machine Learning?”. Ensure it contains at least 10 slides and covers all requested sections (definition, why ML, types, key concepts, workflow, algorithms, evaluation, pitfalls, examples, recap) with speaker notes and visual/diagram suggestions.

quiz_ml_basics.instruction: Output is incomplete: provide exactly 12 questions total: 8 multiple-choice (each with 4 options and clearly marked correct option), 2 true/false, and 2 short-answer. Ensure coverage of required topics (definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, features/labels, training vs inference, overfitting, evaluation metrics, real-world applications). Include a structured answer key that exists at result.output.quiz.answer_key (or equivalent field) and add 1–2 sentence explanations for every question.
